dated_events.title: Room Use Survey System (RUSS) Training
dated_events.description: Join us for a workshop about the Room Use Survey System (RUSS). The RUSS database maintains room-level information within the university facilities, including all Educational and General Use Buildings (E&G) and a few auxiliary buildings. Please see the details below regarding this training.     RUSS Training (60 minutes)  35 minutes – RUSS Data ElementsUnit 1: Overview of room use codesUnit 2: Ove
dated_events.raw_text: Join us for a workshop about the Room Use Survey System (RUSS). The RUSS database maintains room-level information within the university facilities, including all Educational and General Use Buildings (E&G) and a few auxiliary buildings. Please see the details below regarding this training.     RUSS Training (60 minutes)  35 minutes – RUSS Data ElementsUnit 1: Overview of room use codesUnit 2: Overview of room functional categories 20 minutes – RUSS Website Navigation 5 minutes – Q&A   Instructors:   Si Ding Wang (Planning Analyst II, RUSS Data Coordinator, Facilities Management) Brian Culpepper (Data Scientist V, RUSS Tech. Support, Center for Advanced Spatial Technologies)   Audiences:  Building Executives/Alternates, Facilities Managers, Departmental Chairs, and their assistants who have editing access to the RUSS database. Anyone interested in space data.

dated_events.raw_text: Please join us for our monthly Parents and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group Coffee Chats. dated_events.title: Access the full richness of biological complexity with single cell and spatial multiomics
dated_events.description: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hou
dated_events.raw_text: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hours throughout the day to meet with labs and discuss any projects or anything 10X. Grab your appointment spot now! (You must go to May 28th on the calendar to see available options.)  Questions? Please reach out to Ryan.Dohn@10xgenomics.com  The vast complexities of biology require approaches to build a complete picture, starting from single cells to tissues and beyond. At 10x Genomics, we provide single cell, spatial, and in situ innovations that fuel scientific discoveries and drive exponential progress. Unravel highly complex biological systems, while bringing into focus the details that matter most. Join us to learn how Chromium Single Cell, Visium Spatial, and Atera In Situ platforms from 10x Genomics can help you push the boundaries of your research. Uncover molecular insights, dissect cell-type differences, detect novel subtypes and biomarkers, decipher spatial gene expression patterns, and more. By enabling unparalleled insights into cancer, immunology, neuroscience, and developmental biology, 10x Genomics gives researchers the ability to see biology in new ways.

dated_events.title: Cruise to Campus
dated_events.description: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with
dated_events.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua

dated_events.title: Guest Artist Concert: Alan Chow
dated_events.description: Pianist Alan Chow will be in concert at Stella Boyle Smith Concert Hall performing works from Haydn, Liszt, and Chopin.  Previously having served on the faculty of the University of Arkansas and Northwestern University, Mr Chow is currently Professor of Piano and Chair of the Piano Department at the Eastman School of Music.  The performance will be free and open to the public.

dated_events.title: Copy of Open Studio with Carla Edwards, Artist-in-Residence at the School of Art
dated_events.description: Join the School of Art in the Fulbright College of Arts and Sciences at the University of Arkansas for an open studio visit with internationally acclaimed artist Carla Edwards. Carla will be at the School of Art for a monthlong residency this June as part of Handwork: Celebrating American Craft 2026, a national initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Ind
dated_events.raw_text: Join the School of Art in the Fulbright College of Arts and Sciences at the University of Arkansas for an open studio visit with internationally acclaimed artist Carla Edwards. Carla will be at the School of Art for a monthlong residency this June as part of Handwork: Celebrating American Craft 2026, a national initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. The residency will culminate in a public open house from 5-7 p.m. June 23 at the Studio + Design Center with light refreshments and snacks. Visitors will have the opportunity to meet Edwards, view works in progress and engage directly with her artistic practice. The event is free and open to the public. Free parking is available in the lot behind the Sculpture building. The Studio + Design Center is located at 696 W. Praxis Lane, Fayetteville, AR 72701.

dated_events.title: TIAA Webinar: Identity Theft - Safeguarding Your Finances
dated_events.description: Identity theft is a danger for each and every one of us—almost on a daily basis. Whether it’s an email or text that sounds enticing, a suspicious phone call or a social media post with an urgent request from a supposed “friend,” someone may be trying to steal your identity for financial gain.     In this webinar we’ll look at how to:  · Understand the threats  · Know how it can happen  · Secure yo
dated_events.raw_text: Identity theft is a danger for each and every one of us—almost on a daily basis. Whether it’s an email or text that sounds enticing, a suspicious phone call or a social media post with an urgent request from a supposed “friend,” someone may be trying to steal your identity for financial gain.     In this webinar we’ll look at how to:  · Understand the threats  · Know how it can happen  · Secure your information  · Look out for others  · Act quickly if you’re a victim

dated_events.title: Father's Friday Drop-in Celebration and Recognition
dated_events.description: The Parent and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group (PCECRG) invites you to join us for a drop-in celebration and recognition of the fathers in our lives! Yes, we know Father's Day was last Sunday, but fathers exist all year long, so we'll keep the party going into next week.      Our gathering will be a simple one, with lunch at a Union eatery of your choice provided and paid for as
dated_events.raw_text: The Parent and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group (PCECRG) invites you to join us for a drop-in celebration and recognition of the fathers in our lives! Yes, we know Father's Day was last Sunday, but fathers exist all year long, so we'll keep the party going into next week.      Our gathering will be a simple one, with lunch at a Union eatery of your choice provided and paid for as a small token of thanks and appreciation to you, your father, and all of those who parent.     Drop in anytime between 11:30-1:30 to thank a dad, share a story about a dad, be in the presence of dads.      All are welcome.     Please register to indicate your attendance at  this form: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/jMUUM35kx0

dated_events.raw_text: Please join us for our monthly Parents and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group Coffee Chats. dated_events.title: Cruise to Campus
dated_events.description: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with
dated_events.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua

candidates.description: Join us for a workshop about the Room Use Survey System (RUSS). Please see the details below regarding this training.     RUSS Training (60 minutes)  35 minutes – RUSS Data ElementsUnit 1: Overview of room use codesUnit 2: Ove
candidates.raw_text: Join us for a workshop about the Room Use Survey System (RUSS). The RUSS database maintains room-level information within the university facilities, including all Educational and General Use Buildings (E&G) and a few auxiliary buildings. Please see the details below regarding this training.     RUSS Training (60 minutes)  35 minutes – RUSS Data ElementsUnit 1: Overview of room use codesUnit 2: Overview of room functional categories 20 minutes – RUSS Website Navigation 5 minutes – Q&A   Instructors:   Si Ding Wang (Planning Analyst II, RUSS Data Coordinator, Facilities Management) Brian Culpepper (Data Scientist V, RUSS Tech. Support, Center for Advanced Spatial Technologies)   Audiences:  Building Executives/Alternates, Facilities Managers, Departmental Chairs, and their assistants who have editing access to the RUSS database. Anyone interested in space data.

candidates.raw_text: Please join us for our monthly Parents and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group Coffee Chats.  This is a casual, drop-in time for connection (caffeine is optional.) candidates.raw_text: EVENT DATE: June 12, 2026  REGISTRATION DEADLINE: June 5, 2026  EVENT TIME: 08:30 am to 04:30 pm CT  LOCATION: 2 E Center St, Fayetteville AR Room 302 or Online     Mastering Excel is a process, and we're here to guide you. Our live online or in-person classes provide hands-on experience with an expert instructor at every step. Maximize your skills by combining both Excel class levels and concluding with advanced analytics in Power BI or by choosing a single class to quickly move your talent up a notch. Excel gives you the power to organize, analyze, and present data, empowering decision-makers to move forward with confidence.     Designed for new users, this introductory Excel class establishes a fundamental skillset that is critical for success and provides a strong foundation for advanced learning.     LEARN MORE: https://training.uark.edu/portal/training?cmd=catalog&course=microsoft-excel-1

candidates.title: Access the full richness of biological complexity with single cell and spatial multiomics
candidates.description: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hou
candidates.raw_text: 10X Genomics is hosting a Seminar at University of Arkansas Fayetteville on May 28th. Come learn about the latest updates in single cell and spatial transcriptomics, including our newest platform: Atera!  Registration is required as lunch will be provided!   👉 Please use the registration form to confirm your attendance.   Additionally, Scientific Advisor Joe Tolar and I will be hosting office hours throughout the day to meet with labs and discuss any projects or anything 10X. Grab your appointment spot now! (You must go to May 28th on the calendar to see available options.)  Questions? Please reach out to Ryan.Dohn@10xgenomics.com  The vast complexities of biology require approaches to build a complete picture, starting from single cells to tissues and beyond. At 10x Genomics, we provide single cell, spatial, and in situ innovations that fuel scientific discoveries and drive exponential progress. Unravel highly complex biological systems, while bringing into focus the details that matter most. Join us to learn how Chromium Single Cell, Visium Spatial, and Atera In Situ platforms from 10x Genomics can help you push the boundaries of your research. Uncover molecular insights, dissect cell-type differences, detect novel subtypes and biomarkers, decipher spatial gene expression patterns, and more. By enabling unparalleled insights into cancer, immunology, neuroscience, and developmental biology, 10x Genomics gives researchers the ability to see biology in new ways.

candidates.title: Cruise to Campus
candidates.description: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with
candidates.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua

candidates.title: Guest Artist Concert: Alan Chow
candidates.description: Pianist Alan Chow will be in concert at Stella Boyle Smith Concert Hall performing works from Haydn, Liszt, and Chopin.  Previously having served on the faculty of the University of Arkansas and Northwestern University, Mr Chow is currently Professor of Piano and Chair of the Piano Department at the Eastman School of Music.  The performance will be free and open to the public.

candidates.title: Copy of Open Studio with Carla Edwards, Artist-in-Residence at the School of Art
candidates.description: Join the School of Art in the Fulbright College of Arts and Sciences at the University of Arkansas for an open studio visit with internationally acclaimed artist Carla Edwards. Carla will be at the School of Art for a monthlong residency this June as part of Handwork: Celebrating American Craft 2026, a national initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Ind
candidates.raw_text: Join the School of Art in the Fulbright College of Arts and Sciences at the University of Arkansas for an open studio visit with internationally acclaimed artist Carla Edwards. Carla will be at the School of Art for a monthlong residency this June as part of Handwork: Celebrating American Craft 2026, a national initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. The residency will culminate in a public open house from 5-7 p.m. June 23 at the Studio + Design Center with light refreshments and snacks. Visitors will have the opportunity to meet Edwards, view works in progress and engage directly with her artistic practice. The event is free and open to the public. Free parking is available in the lot behind the Sculpture building. The Studio + Design Center is located at 696 W. Praxis Lane, Fayetteville, AR 72701.

candidates.title: TIAA Webinar: Identity Theft - Safeguarding Your Finances
candidates.description: Identity theft is a danger for each and every one of us—almost on a daily basis. Whether it’s an email or text that sounds enticing, a suspicious phone call or a social media post with an urgent request from a supposed “friend,” someone may be trying to steal your identity for financial gain.     In this webinar we’ll look at how to:  · Understand the threats  · Know how it can happen  · Secure yo
candidates.raw_text: Identity theft is a danger for each and every one of us—almost on a daily basis. Whether it’s an email or text that sounds enticing, a suspicious phone call or a social media post with an urgent request from a supposed “friend,” someone may be trying to steal your identity for financial gain.     In this webinar we’ll look at how to:  · Understand the threats  · Know how it can happen  · Secure your information  · Look out for others  · Act quickly if you’re a victim

candidates.title: Father's Friday Drop-in Celebration and Recognition
candidates.description: The Parent and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group (PCECRG) invites you to join us for a drop-in celebration and recognition of the fathers in our lives! Yes, we know Father's Day was last Sunday, but fathers exist all year long, so we'll keep the party going into next week.      Our gathering will be a simple one, with lunch at a Union eatery of your choice provided and paid for as
candidates.raw_text: The Parent and Caregivers Employee and Colleagues Resource Group (PCECRG) invites you to join us for a drop-in celebration and recognition of the fathers in our lives! Yes, we know Father's Day was last Sunday, but fathers exist all year long, so we'll keep the party going into next week.      Our gathering will be a simple one, with lunch at a Union eatery of your choice provided and paid for as a small token of thanks and appreciation to you, your father, and all of those who parent.     Drop in anytime between 11:30-1:30 to thank a dad, share a story about a dad, be in the presence of dads.      All are welcome.     Please register to indicate your attendance at  this form: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/jMUUM35kx0

candidates.title: Cruise to Campus
candidates.description: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with
candidates.raw_text: The Office for Sustainability invites UARK students, faculty, and staff to join us for Cruise to Campus Wednesday, happening the first Wednesday of each month from February - December. UARK affiliates are encouraged to ride their bikes to campus and join us for breakfast and community on Old Main Lawn. Participants are welcome to enjoy complimentary beverages and breakfast from Onyx, connect with other riders, and learn about the bike resources the University of Arkansas has to offer.     This is a rain or shine event, so long as there is no lighting or thunder. If severe weather occurs, events will be postponed to a later date. For the most up to date information, check out the OFS Instagram @sustainua
